name: OData entity readable without object-level authorization (BOLA / IDOR)
description: Requests a single OData entity by key — /odata/Orders(1) — with no credentials. When the service returns the full entity (an OData JSON envelope carrying the object's fields) to an unauthenticated caller, object-level authorization is missing: any caller can read any object by iterating the integer key. This is the OData shape of OWASP API1:2023 (Broken Object Level Authorization). A single anonymous probe is an INDICATOR, not proof — confirm the cross-tenant read with two identities (`bowire scan --auth-header ... --auth-header-b ...`), which reads the same object as identity A then identity B. The predicate deliberately looks for an owner/identity-bearing field in the body so a public, ownerless lookup table doesn't trip it.

vulnerability.remediation: Enforce object-level authorization on every keyed OData request, not just at the route level. ASP.NET Core OData: resolve the caller identity in the controller/endpoint and filter the query to objects the caller owns (`.Where(o => o.OwnerId == currentUserId)`) before returning — never trust the client-supplied key alone. Add an authorization policy that runs per-object, and cover it with a two-identity test (identity B must NOT read identity A's object). Verify with `bowire scan --auth-header 'Authorization: Bearer <A>' --auth-header-b 'Authorization: Bearer <B>'`.
